discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +2.6%, AVGO/TSM +5%) on SK Hynix record earnings and AI memory demand. VIX up 4% despite rally suggests hedging into Iran/Hormuz headlines. Mid-cap dispersion is the real story: CALX crushed -14% on likely earnings miss, MANH/VECO/WEX gapping up, and BBY weak against a positive tape.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% on SK Hynix record profit and AI memory pricing strength
ITA -1.77% weak despite Iran war backdrop — possibly profit-taking on defense after extended run
XLF flat/red despite bank earnings beats (HBAN) — muted reaction suggests crowded long
VIX +4% alongside SPY +1% — unusual; hedging into Hormuz/oil headline risk

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Likely post-earnings reaction (Calix typically reports late April). 14% drop on 4x volume with no news in feed means Michael needs to verify the print and, critically, guidance language. Small-cap broadband equipment names have been volatile on BEAD program uncertainty.
discoveries.catalyst: Presumed Q1 earnings/guidance — not confirmed in provided news
research_pointers: Confirm Calix Q1 print timing and actual numbers vs consensus
Check guide for Q2 and any commentary on BEAD/rural broadband spending cadence
Compare to EXTR -5.8% same day — is this a networking/edge spending sympathy move or company-specific?
Check short interest and whether -13% breaks prior technical support
discoveries.what_would_falsify: An actual in-line print with conservative-but-achievable guidance would suggest overdone; a genuine miss with cut guidance would make this rational.

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Possible sympathy drag from CALX on networking/edge capex concerns, against a tape where SMH is +2.6%. Divergence from semis suggests the weakness is enterprise-networking specific rather than broad tech.
discoveries.catalyst: No direct news — likely CALX read-through or pre-earnings positioning
research_pointers: EXTR earnings date — are they reporting imminently?
Check if CALX commentary (once read) bears on service-provider vs enterprise networking — if CALX issue is rural broadband-specific, EXTR drag may be misplaced
Cross-reference with ANET, JNPR, CSCO action for sector read
discoveries.what_would_falsify: CALX guidance cut specifically cites enterprise networking weakness, validating EXTR concern.

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: Weak against a +1% tape and +2.6% AAPL with no obvious catalyst. Possible concerns: tariff refund headline implies prior tariff-hit consumer electronics pricing resets, possible analyst downgrade, or consumer spending worry tied to CNBC survey showing Trump economic approval at lows. Volume only 1.2x — not a panic, more a grind.
discoveries.catalyst: Unclear — possibly tariff-refund news interpreted as removing pricing tailwind, or consumer sentiment data
research_pointers: Check for analyst actions on BBY this morning
Did the Supreme Court tariff refund ruling create a read-through for consumer electronics retailers (were they benefiting from tariff-driven pricing)?
Check BBY's holiday/Q1 commentary and compare to recent same-store trends at peers
GME +4.9% vs BBY -4.6% — meme flow rotation only, or real?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A specific downgrade or guide cut would make it rational; absence of identifiable catalyst with volume only 1.2x suggests positioning/technical rather than fundamental.

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Down 6% on only 1.2x volume against XBI +0.67% — idiosyncratic weakness in bioprocessing. No news in feed. Bioprocessing names have been whipsawed by biotech capex commentary.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — check for pre-announcement, analyst action, or peer commentary (Danaher, Sartorius)
research_pointers: RGEN earnings date proximity — is this pre-announcement risk?
Check Sartorius/Danaher bioprocessing commentary this week
Low volume multiple suggests this may be a single-holder unwind rather than news-driven
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Identified peer negative read-through or guide cut.

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Supply chain software name up 10.9% over five days with today's move on 2x volume — consistent with post-earnings momentum or positive guidance signal. MANH has historically had clean beats.
research_pointers: Confirm MANH earnings date and results
Check RPO/bookings commentary — Street watches cloud subscription growth closely
Valuation check — MANH typically trades rich; verify multiple re-rating room
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Move driven by acquisition rumor or single-day flow without fundamental follow-through.

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments
discoveries.mechanism: Semi-cap equipment sympathy to SK Hynix record memory profit and HBM demand — Veeco sells MOCVD/ion-beam tools used in advanced packaging and compound semi. +18% over five days suggests trend, not one-day spike.
discoveries.catalyst: SK Hynix record Q1, AI memory demand, AVGO/TSM strength
research_pointers: Check VECO exposure to HBM/advanced packaging capex
Compare to ACLS, ONTO, KLIC for sector conviction
Earnings date — is the run setting up for a sell-the-news?
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Disappointing guide at upcoming print despite strong memory backdrop would signal company-specific issue.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix record Q1 profit on AI memory demand; AVGO +5%, TSM +5%

secondary_opportunities.relationship: unrelated — flagged because its weakness is NOT chain-explained

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump attacks SCOTUS over $159B tariff refund ruling and upcoming birthright citizenship case; no new tariff threat.
trump_signals.note: Post is venting about a ruling already priced in. Indirect read: if importers/retailers receive tariff refunds, it's a one-time cash benefit to consumer electronics, auto parts, and apparel importers. BBY weakness today runs counter to this — worth checking if the market is pricing a removal of tariff-driven pricing power rather than the refund windfall. Not a direct tradable Trump signal.

discovery.no_signals_note: Earnings-dominated tape. Several flagged names (CALX, MANH, RGEN) likely have earnings prints not present in the provided news feed — Michael should verify before acting. CALX -14% is the highest-conviction 'something happened, go look' pointer.

ai_announcements.headline: Google updates Workspace, Chrome, Gmail, and launches Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform + new TPUs
ai_announcements.summary: Google announced a broad AI push: Workspace Intelligence (AI as office intern), Chrome auto-browse for enterprise, Gmail AI Overviews, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, new TPUs competing with Nvidia, and AI in Maps.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan's supply chain/WMS software is specialized vertical software; generic Google enterprise AI doesn't replace it. AI agents may actually accelerate demand for structured supply chain data platforms.
research_pointers: Move likely driven by own earnings/guidance rather than Google Cloud news - verify earnings date
Check if any Google Cloud Next showcase included Manhattan as partner

ai_announcements.headline: ServiceNow stock craters 13% on Armis acquisition profit headwinds
ai_announcements.summary: ServiceNow reported 22% revenue growth but stock fell >13% after-hours on Armis acquisition concerns. Not directly AI-related but relevant for enterprise software sympathy.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI partners with Infosys for enterprise AI (software dev, legacy modernization, DevOps)
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI-Infosys tie-up targets software engineering and legacy modernization.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Biggest movers (CALX -14%, EXTR -5.8%, RGEN -6.25%, BBY -4.6%, VECO +5.3%, WEX +4.2%) appear driven by company-specific earnings/guidance, not today's AI announcements. CALX and EXTR are networking equipment - no direct AI announcement catalyst in the news set; likely earnings-driven. RGEN is bioprocessing (not AI-exposed to today's news). No high-conviction AI-driven mispricings identified. Quiet night from an AI-overreaction standpoint despite heavy Google Cloud Next news flow.
